title: Tomoya Hosoda elected as world's first openly transgender man in public office
description: Tomoya Hosoda won a seat on the city council of Iruma, Japan, making him the first openly transgender man anywhere to hold elected public office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Candice Jackson takes senior civil-rights post at US Education Department
description: Candice Jackson became the first openly gay person appointed Deputy Assistant Secretary for Strategic Operations and Outreach in the US Department of Education's Office for Civil Rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alaska's first openly LGBT officials elected to Anchorage Assembly
description: Christopher Constant and Felix Rivera won seats on the Anchorage Assembly, becoming the first openly LGBT elected officials in the state of Alaska.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andy Street becomes the UK's first openly gay directly elected metro mayor
description: Andy Street took office as metro mayor, the first openly gay person to win a directly elected metro mayoralty in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Leo Varadkar becomes Ireland's first openly gay Taoiseach
description: Leo Varadkar was chosen as leader of Fine Gael with 51 of 73 parliamentary-party votes and took office as Taoiseach. He was Ireland's first openly gay head of government, its youngest, and the first of half-Indian descent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Paul Feinman becomes first openly gay judge on New York's highest court
description: Paul Feinman joined the New York Court of Appeals as its first openly gay judge.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ana Brnabic becomes Serbia's first openly gay prime minister
description: Ana Brnabic took office as prime minister of Serbia, the first openly gay person and the first woman to hold the post. She later marched in Belgrade Pride, the first head of government of any Balkan country to attend a pride event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alice Weidel elected AfD co-chair and Bundestag member
description: Alice Weidel, who is openly gay, became the first woman and first openly gay person to co-chair the Alternative for Germany party and won a seat in the German federal parliament the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Danica Roem elected as Virginia's first transgender lawmaker
description: Danica Roem won election to the Virginia legislature, becoming the state's first transgender lawmaker and drawing national attention as a milestone for trans representation in US state politics.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andrea Jenkins elected to Minneapolis City Council
description: Andrea Jenkins won a seat on the Minneapolis City Council, making her the first openly transgender Black woman elected to public office in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly transgender men elected to US public office
description: Tyler Titus won a seat on the Erie School Board in Pennsylvania and Phillipe Cunningham won a Minneapolis City Council seat on the same night, making them the first two openly transgender men elected to public office in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Allison Ikley-Freeman elected as Oklahoma's first lesbian lawmaker
description: Allison Ikley-Freeman won a legislative seat in Oklahoma, becoming the state's first openly lesbian lawmaker.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Palm Springs elects America's first all-LGBT city council
description: Voters in Palm Springs, California elected a city council composed entirely of LGBT members — three gay men, a transgender woman and a bisexual woman — a first for any US city.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taiwan's Constitutional Court rules the same-sex marriage ban unconstitutional
description: Taiwan's Constitutional Court held that the Civil Code's exclusion of same-sex couples violated the constitutional guarantees of freedom of marriage and equality, giving the legislature two years to amend the law — after which same-sex couples could register marriages with full legal status.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denmark removes transgender identities from mental-disorder classification
description: Denmark struck transgender identities from its national list of mental health disorders, the second country in the world to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Unitarian Universalists adopt gender-inclusive language in bylaws
description: The Unitarian Universalist Association's General Assembly voted to replace the phrase "men and women" with "people" in its bylaws, adopting inclusive wording that covers non-binary, genderqueer, gender-fluid, agender, intersex, two-spirit and polygender members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Philippa York comes out as transgender
description: Former professional cyclist Philippa York announced her transition, becoming the first former professional cyclist to have publicly transitioned.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ines Rau becomes first openly transgender Playboy Playmate
description: Model Ines Rau was named a Playboy Playmate, the first openly transgender woman to hold the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US military health system pays for gender-affirming surgery for the first time
description: The US Defense Health Agency approved payment for sex reassignment surgery for an active-duty soldier for the first time. The operation, deemed medically necessary by the treating doctor, took place on 14 November at a private hospital because military hospitals lacked the surgical expertise.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: LGBT Melton opens as first LGBTQ+ youth service in Melton Mowbray
description: LGBT Melton, established on 21 January by Anthony Marvin, opened on 6 March as the first youth provision in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire aimed specifically at LGBTQ+ young people. The service later extended across Leicestershire and Rutland.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jersey legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The Crown dependency of Jersey passed a same-sex marriage law, which took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Alderney
description: Marriage between same-sex partners became legal in Alderney, in the Channel Islands.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Bermuda
description: Following a Supreme Court decision, same-sex marriage came into effect in the British overseas territory of Bermuda.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Costa Rica's Supreme Court orders legalization of same-sex marriage
description: Costa Rica's Supreme Court ruled in favor of same-sex marriage, with the decision set to take effect by 26 May 2020.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trinidad and Tobago decriminalizes same-sex intimacy
description: Consensual same-sex acts were decriminalized in Trinidad and Tobago after the High Court struck down the country's buggery laws.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: India decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: India's Supreme Court struck down the colonial-era Section 377 as applied to consensual same-sex acts, decriminalizing homosexuality in the world's second most populous country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK prime minister apologizes for colonial anti-LGBT laws
description: Theresa May expressed deep regret for Britain's role in exporting laws criminalizing LGBT people across the Commonwealth and for the discrimination they left behind. At the time, 36 of 53 Commonwealth countries still retained colonial-era criminalization laws.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay college football player scores a touchdown
description: Wyatt Pertuset became the first openly gay college football player to score a touchdown.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Winter Olympics with openly gay male competitors
description: The Pyeongchang Games marked the first time in Winter Olympic history that openly gay male athletes competed.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Silvia Vasquez-Lavado completes the Seven Summits
description: Mountaineer Silvia Vasquez-Lavado became the first openly gay woman to climb the highest mountain on every continent, completing both the Messner and Bass versions of the Seven Summits.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Antarctica's first Pride celebration held at McMurdo Station
description: Around 30 to 40 researchers, including 12 who identified as LGBT, held a Pride celebration at a bar in McMurdo Station — the first pride event on the Antarctic continent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stav Strashko earns first Ophir Award nomination for an openly trans actress
description: Stav Strashko became the first openly transgender actress nominated for the Ophir Award for Best Actress, Israel's national film prize.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sandra Lawson ordained as first openly gay Black female rabbi
description: Sandra Lawson was ordained as a rabbi, becoming the first openly gay Black woman in the world to hold the office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Adam Rippon becomes first openly gay US Winter Olympian and medalist
description: Figure skater Adam Rippon was the first openly gay athlete to qualify for a US Winter Olympic team, and his bronze in the team event made him the first openly gay American to win a Winter Olympics medal.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jessica Platt comes out as first trans woman in North American pro hockey
description: Canadian Women's Hockey League player Jessica Platt came out as a transgender woman, a first for professional hockey in North America.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First active-duty same-sex couple marries at West Point
description: Daniel Hall and Vinny Franchino became the first active-duty same-sex couple to marry at the US Military Academy at West Point.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Yance Ford becomes first openly trans director nominated for an Academy Award
description: Yance Ford, nominated with Joslyn Barnes for Best Documentary Feature for Strong Island, which Ford also directed, was the first openly transgender man and first openly transgender director to receive an Academy Award nomination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laverne Cox is first openly trans person on a Cosmopolitan cover
description: Laverne Cox appeared on the cover of Cosmopolitan South Africa's February issue, the first openly transgender person on the cover of any Cosmopolitan edition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Paris Lees becomes first openly trans woman featured in British Vogue
description: Journalist Paris Lees was the first openly transgender woman to be featured in British Vogue.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Holland debuts as first openly gay K-pop singer
description: Holland released his first single, "Neverland", debuting as the first openly gay K-pop singer.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dee Rees is first queer Black woman nominated for an Oscar writing award
description: Dee Rees, a lesbian filmmaker, was nominated with Virgil Williams for the Academy Award for Best Adapted Screenplay for Mudbound, making her the first queer Black woman nominated in any Academy Award writing category.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rachel Morrison earns first Best Cinematography Oscar nomination for a woman
description: Cinematographer Rachel Morrison became the first woman — and, as a lesbian, the first lesbian — nominated for the Academy Award for Best Cinematography.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Eric Radford becomes first openly gay man to win Winter Olympic gold
description: Canadian figure skater Eric Radford became the first openly gay man to win a gold medal at any Winter Olympics.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay pastor consecrated in the Colombian Methodist Church
description: Jhon Botia Miranda was consecrated as a pastor by Bishop Juan Cardona, the first openly gay pastor in the Colombian Methodist Church and in the Methodist Church anywhere in Latin America.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Love, Simon is first major-studio film centered on a gay teenage romance
description: Love, Simon premiered as the first film from a major studio to focus on a gay teenage romance.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Todd Harrity comes out as first openly gay male pro squash player
description: Todd Harrity came out as gay, becoming the first openly gay professional male squash player in the world.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UFC 224 is first UFC event headlined by two openly gay fighters
description: Raquel Pennington faced Amanda Nunes for the UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship at UFC 224, the first event in UFC history headlined by two openly gay fighters; Pennington lost by TKO in the fifth round.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Vatican uses the acronym LGBT in an official document for the first time
description: For the first time, an official Vatican document included the acronym LGBT.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sue Bird and Megan Rapinoe are first same-sex couple on ESPN's Body Issue
description: Athletes Sue Bird and Megan Rapinoe appeared together on the cover of ESPN's Body Issue, the first same-sex couple to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sharon Afek becomes the Israel Defense Forces' first openly gay major general
description: Sharon Afek was promoted to major general, the first openly gay officer to reach that rank in the Israel Defense Forces.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Beth Ford becomes first openly gay woman to lead a Fortune 500 company
description: Land O'Lakes named Beth Ford as its first female CEO, making her the first openly gay woman to run a Fortune 500 company.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
